entities.name: ARECANUT
entities.description: The crop affected by fruit rot disease in India

entities.name: FRUIT ROT
entities.description: A devastating disease of arecanut caused by oomycetous fungi

entities.name: PHYTOPHTHORA MEADII
entities.description: The specific oomycetous fungus identified as the predominant cause of fruit rot in arecanut

entities.name: KARNATAKA
entities.description: The Indian state where the arecanut fruit rot samples were collected

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study on arecanut fruit rot was conducted

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: The study of physical characteristics like colony morphology and sporangia to characterize the pathogen

entities.name: MULTI-GENE PHYLOGENY
entities.description: The use of multiple gene sequences to determine the evolutionary relationship and identity of the pathogen

entities.name: HAPLOTYPE ANALYSIS
entities.description: The analysis of genetic variations to identify haplotypes and population diversity

entities.name: ITS
entities.description: Internal Transcribed Spacer, a gene marker used for phylogenetic identification

entities.name: BETA-TUBULIN
entities.description: The beta-tubulin gene marker used in multi-gene phylogeny

entities.name: TEF-1ALPHA
entities.description: The translation elongation factor 1-alpha gene marker used in multi-gene phylogeny

entities.name: COX-II
entities.description: The cytochrome c oxidase subunit II gene marker used in multi-gene phylogeny

entities.name: HAPLOTYPES
entities.description: Genetic variants used to represent the diversity of the pathogen population

entities.name: POPULATION DYNAMICS
entities.description: The study of the changes and distribution of the pathogen population over time and space

entities.name: DISEASE MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES
entities.description: Approaches developed to curtail the occurrence and spread of fruit rot in the arecanut ecosystem

entities.name: SOUTH-WEST MONSOON
entities.description: The climatic period during which the infected samples were collected
